article_meta.title: IdeasWhat History Says Will Happen Next in IranMilitant groups have become more violent, specifically against civilians,

reasoning: The model identifies three instances of potential bias in the author's prose. All three are drawn from the author's own voice, not from quoted sources, so no attribution rule violations occur. The instances are defensible but some are debatable. The 'Bias by Omission' flag citing 'The entire article structure' is reasonable for an opinion/analysis piece but somewhat questionable since this is explicitly an opinion/analysis column (labeled 'Ideas' from The Atlantic) where presenting one thesis is expected. The 'Slant' flag on 'the record shows' is defensible as it presents a contested claim as settled. The 'perhaps understandable' flag is debatable - it could be read as dismissive but also as a fair acknowledgment. The bias type labels are mostly reasonable though some could be debated.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All three flagged instances are from the author's own prose. No quoted text or attributed statements were flagged. Perfect compliance with the attribution rule.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: The 'perhaps understandable' flag is debatable - it's a hedging phrase that could be read either as dismissive or as a reasonable qualifier. The 'entire article structure' as biased text is somewhat vague for a Bias by Omission flag. The 'record shows' flag is the strongest of the three. Some items are questionable given this is an opinion/analysis piece.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model could have flagged 'Not for nothing' as informal/loaded author prose, or the characterization of subordinates as 'undisciplined hotheads' (though this paraphrases a source). The author's framing of Soleimani's 'tactical restraint' as positive could also be flagged. Some potential instances were missed but the main ones were caught.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: The 'Slant' label for the 'record shows' statement could arguably be 'Opinion Statements Presented as Fact' instead, since it presents a contested claim as established. 'Bias by Omission' for the overall structure is defensible. 'Subjective Qualifying Adjectives' for 'perhaps understandable' is a stretch - this is more of a framing/tone issue than an adjective issue. Labels are in the right neighborhood but not perfectly applied.
